record.target_claim: Greedy/inductive algorithm works: given S with |S| <= sqrt(2) and z with |z| <= 1, either |S+z| <= sqrt(2) or |S-z| <= sqrt(2).
record.candidate_counterexample: S = sqrt(2) (real), z = i (purely imaginary, |z|=1). Then |S+z| = |S-z| = sqrt(3) > sqrt(2).
record.impact: Simple greedy induction with no ordering does not work. Need a different proof strategy or a clever ordering.
